P1: Fail closed when Redis blacklist initialization fails in production; silently falling back to in-memory revocation weakens token revocation guarantees across instances.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lib/security/jwt-auth.ts, line 545: Fail closed when Redis blacklist initialization fails in production; silently falling back to in-memory revocation weakens token revocation guarantees across instances. @@ -529,10 +529,30 @@ export class InMemoryTokenBlacklist implements TokenBlacklistProvider { + console.log('[Security] Using Redis token blacklist for distributed revocation'); + return blacklist; + } catch (error) { + console.warn('[Security] Failed to initialize Redis blacklist, falling back to in-memory:', error); + } + } ```
```suggestion console.error('[Security] Failed to initialize Redis blacklist in production:', error); throw error; ```

<!-- metadata:{"confidence":6} -->

-            }

P1: Rollback version lookup dropped `filesChanged` fallback, so versions returned by `listVersions()` can no longer be rolled back when `workspaceVersion` is null.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lib/virtual-filesystem/git-backed-vfs.ts, line 277: Rollback version lookup dropped `filesChanged` fallback, so versions returned by `listVersions()` can no longer be rolled back when `workspaceVersion` is null. @@ -271,10 +271,10 @@ export class GitBackedVFS { - // Match by version or commit metadata - return c.filesChanged === targetVersion || + // Match by explicit workspace version (reliable version tracking) + return c.workspaceVersion === targetVersion || c.message?.includes(`v${targetVersion}`); }); ```

P2: `workspaceVersion` is read from a field that isn’t selected in the Supabase query, so this value is always `null` in commit history results.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lib/orchestra/stateful-agent/commit/shadow-commit.ts, line 311: `workspaceVersion` is read from a field that isn’t selected in the Supabase query, so this value is always `null` in commit history results. @@ -307,6 +308,7 @@ export class ShadowCommitManager { author: d.author, createdAt: d.created_at, filesChanged: 0, // Would need to query files separately + workspaceVersion: d.workspace_version ?? null, })); } ```

P2: `totalTokens` is falling back to `completion_tokens` instead of `total_tokens`, which can underreport token usage in the `done` event.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lib/api/streaming-events.ts, line 163: `totalTokens` is falling back to `completion_tokens` instead of `total_tokens`, which can underreport token usage in the `done` event. @@ -139,29 +139,38 @@ export function createStreamingEvents( requestId, success: response.success, - totalTokens: response.data?.usage?.totalTokens || content?.length || 0, + totalTokens: response.data?.usage?.totalTokens ?? response.data?.usage?.completion_tokens ?? 0, qualityScore: response.data?.qualityScore, source: response.source, ```
```suggestion totalTokens: response.data?.usage?.totalTokens ?? response.data?.usage?.total_tokens ?? response.data?.usage?.completion_tokens ?? 0, ```

P2: This check compares workspace version to per-file versions, which is not a valid ordering check and causes false "newer edits" detection (especially delete-only transactions).
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At lib/virtual-filesystem/filesystem-edit-session-service.ts, line 324: This check compares workspace version to per-file versions, which is not a valid ordering check and causes false "newer edits" detection (especially delete-only transactions). @@ -304,18 +304,32 @@ class FilesystemEditSessionService { + // If current version > transaction's highest version, other edits exist + const transactionTip = Math.max(...tx.operations.map((op) => op.newVersion)); + + if (currentVersion > transactionTip) { + // Workspace has been modified since this transaction - manual revert is safer + logger.warn('[FilesystemEditSession] Workspace has newer edits, using manual revert to avoid collateral damage', { ```

**`messageListener` is out of scope in `finally`.**

It is declared inside the `try` block (line 239), but the `finally` block (line 265) references it (line 268). With JavaScript/TypeScript block scoping, `const` declarations are not accessible outside their block. Move the declaration before the `try` block so it's accessible in `finally`.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@lib/api/v2-gateway-client.ts` around lines 238 - 250, The variable
messageListener is declared inside the try block but referenced in the finally
block (used for cleanup with redis.off/removeListener), causing a scope error;
move the declaration of messageListener (the function assigned and passed to
redis.on('pmessage')) to before the try so it is in the outer scope, then keep
the current redis.on('pmessage', messageListener) inside the try and use the
same messageListener in the finally to remove the listener.
```

</details>

**Handle per-command pipeline errors explicitly in `isRevokedMultiple` and `revokeMultiple`.**

Pipeline commands can fail individually, and the current code silently treats errors as `false` (not revoked), allowing revoked tokens through. Both methods must check for per-command errors since `ioredis` returns `[err, result]` tuples for each command.

<details>
<summary>🔒 Handle per-command pipeline errors explicitly</summary>

```diff
     const results = await pipeline.exec();
+    if (!results) {
+      throw new Error('Redis pipeline returned no results');
+    }
     const statusMap = new Map<string, boolean>();
 
     jtis.forEach((jti, index) => {
-      const exists = results?.[index]?.[1] as number;
+      const [err, exists] = results[index] ?? [];
+      if (err) throw err;
       statusMap.set(jti, exists === 1);
     });
```

Apply the same error checking pattern to `revokeMultiple` (line 115).
</details>

<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@lib/security/redis-token-blacklist.ts` around lines 133 - 139, The pipeline
result handling in isRevokedMultiple and revokeMultiple must inspect each
per-command tuple returned by pipeline.exec (ioredis returns [err, result] per
command) instead of assuming results[index][1] is valid; update
isRevokedMultiple to iterate results and for each entry check const [err, res] =
results[index]; if err is non-null, log the error and conservatively treat that
jti as revoked (statusMap.set(jti, true)) or otherwise surface the error,
otherwise set statusMap.set(jti, (res === 1)); apply the same per-command error
checking pattern in revokeMultiple (for revokeMultiple, surface or throw on
per-command write errors rather than silently ignoring them) so failures are not
treated as successful operations.
```

/**
 * Helper to add anonymous session cookie to response
 * Call this for ALL routes that use resolveFilesystemOwner
 */
export function withAnonSessionCookie<T extends NextResponse>(
  response: T,
  owner: FilesystemOwnerResolution
): T {
  if (owner.anonSessionId) {
    response.cookies.set('anon-session-id', owner.anonSessionId, {
      path: '/',
      maxAge: 31536000,
      sameSite: 'lax',
      httpOnly: true,
      secure: true,
    });
  }
  return response;
}
```

#!/bin/bash

echo "=== Finding which packages depend on glob in package-lock.json ==="
# Extract the structure to find direct dependents of glob
python3 << 'PYTHON_EOF'
import json

with open('package-lock.json', 'r') as f:
    lock = json.load(f)

# Find all packages that depend on glob
glob_dependents = []
if 'packages' in lock:
    for pkg_name, pkg_info in lock['packages'].items():
        if 'dependencies' in pkg_info and 'glob' in pkg_info['dependencies']:
            version_constraint = pkg_info['dependencies']['glob']
            glob_dependents.append((pkg_name, version_constraint))

print("Packages with glob dependencies:")
for pkg, ver in sorted(glob_dependents):
    print(f"  {pkg}: {ver}")
PYTHON_EOF

echo -e "\n=== Checking pnpm-lock.yaml for glob versions ==="
grep "glob@" pnpm-lock.yaml | head -20
```

**Remove or fix the ineffective `glob` override—transitive dependencies block v11.**

The override specifies `glob: ">=11.0.0"` but npm cannot satisfy it due to conflicting version constraints: `archiver-utils` (v5.0.2) requires `glob ^10.0.0` and `sucrase` (v3.35.0) requires `glob ^10.3.10`. The actual installed version is **glob v10.5.0**, not v11+. Either remove the override if v10 is acceptable, or update the transitive dependencies to support glob v11+ (only `e2b` v2.13.0 declares compatibility with glob ^11.1.0).

**Stale closure: ref can lag behind applyDiffsToFilesystem when sessionId changes.**

The messages effect at lines 564–607 depends only on `[messages]` and calls `applyDiffsRef.current()`. The ref itself is updated in a separate effect at lines 1063–1065 with dependency `[applyDiffsToFilesystem]`. When `messages` and `filesystemSessionId` change in the same render, the callback is recreated with a new sessionId, but the messages effect runs first using the ref's previous value. This means diffs are applied with a stale sessionId. Either add `applyDiffsToFilesystem` as a dependency to the messages effect (once the replay issue is fixed) or synchronize the ref update ahead of the consumer.

`In Next.js App Router, are `"use client"` components still prerendered on the server during the initial request, and is accessing `window` during render supported?`

💡 Result:

In Next.js App Router, "use client" components are prerendered on the server during the initial request to generate static HTML for fast first paint and SEO. They are then hydrated on the client for interactivity. Accessing window (or other browser APIs) directly during the render phase is not supported because the server environment lacks window, leading to "window is not defined" errors. Use useEffect, useLayoutEffect, or checks like typeof window !== 'undefined' to access browser APIs safely after hydration on the client.

Citations:

- 1: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components
- 2: https://nextjs.org/docs/14/app/building-your-application/rendering/client-components
- 3: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/50864
- 4: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75692116/next-js-13-window-is-not-defined

**`pnpm install --prod` on line 50 will cause the build to fail.**

The `pnpm build` command runs `tsc`, which requires the `typescript` package. However, `typescript` is in `devDependencies`, not `dependencies`. The `--prod` flag installs only production dependencies, so TypeScript will not be available, and the build step will fail.

Remove `--prod` from line 50:

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@Dockerfile.worker` at line 50, The RUN instruction that currently executes
"pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod && pnpm build" will omit devDependencies
(including typescript) and break the subsequent build; update the RUN step that
invokes pnpm to remove the "--prod" flag so that devDependencies are installed
before running "pnpm build" (i.e., run pnpm install with --frozen-lockfile only,
then pnpm build).
```

</details>
